problem: src/output/pdf/detail_modules/indicators.rs rendered a detected technology's category via format!("{:?}", tech.category) — Rust's Debug output — instead of a human-readable label, so the PDF's tech-stack table showed raw enum identifiers (e.g. "StaticSiteGenerator") rather than reader-facing text ("Static Site Generator").
evidence: Found while auditing indicators.rs for the tech-stack severity locale leak in the same file; fixed by adding TechCategory::label() (src/tech_stack/mod.rs) with an explicit reader-facing string per variant, and calling tech.category.label().to_string() instead of the Debug format.
expected: No PDF-visible text is ever produced via {:?} Debug formatting of an internal enum — every category/kind/variant that reaches a report surface has an explicit label() (or equivalent) method.
regression: src/tech_stack/mod.rs::tech_category_label_is_a_readable_string_not_the_debug_identifier (asserts label() output differs from Debug output for the multi-word variants where the distinction is meaningful: Cms, StaticSiteGenerator, Ecommerce, JsLibrary).
counter_examples: TechCategory::Framework's label() ("Framework") coincidentally equals its own Debug output — this is not a bug and must not be asserted against generically; the test only checks the variants where Debug and label() are meant to differ.
